summary:
I examined the entire `.claude/` machinery under `/Users/thomasnicklin/Desktop/next-supabase-agent-harness/template/base/` — all 5 hooks plus `lib/hookio.mjs`, `settings.json` and `settings.local.json.example`, `statusline.mjs`, the 7 subagents, 3 slash commands, 3 always-loaded rules, the `authoring-vertical-slice` skill (SKILL.md + 4 progressive-disclosure references + `scaffold-slice.mjs`), and the `CLAUDE.md` kernel. I also read the two load-bearing files the Stop hook and gate consume (`tools/harness.config.mjs`, `tools/validate.mjs`) because the hook contract is meaningless without them.

The architecture is a deterministic "green gate" enforcement kernel built on Claude Code's hook lifecycle. Hook I/O is centralized in `hookio.mjs`, which encodes a three-tier exit-code contract: `pass()` = exit 0 (proceed); `denyTool()` = exit 0 + JSON `hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision:"deny"` (PreToolUse structured refusal with a machine-readable reason); `block()` = exit 2 + stderr (stderr is fed back to the model). PreToolUse hooks are gatekeepers: `pretool-bash-guard` is a regex tripwire that denies dangerous shell (`rm -rf`, force-push, `reset --hard`, `DROP TABLE`, fork bomb) and secret leaks (service-role key, `.env` reads, server-side `getSession`); `pretool-write-guard` blocks invariant-violating file *content* before it lands AND provides tamper-evidence (a root-anchored PROTECTED list of gate/config/CI files that require `HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT=1` to touch). PostToolUse hooks are feedback: `posttool-fast-check` runs Biome `--write` on the single changed file (non-blocking), `posttool-source-check` flags decision sites lacking a `// SOURCE:` provenance comment (blocking, exit 2). The `stop-validate-gate` is the unbreakable finale: it runs `STOP_HOOK_STEPS` from the harness config directly (never through pnpm-script names) and exits 2 with the failures until the build is green.

Defense-in-depth is layered: settings.json `permissions.deny` (Layer 1) protects the hooks, settings, `.mcp.json`, and `.harness/`; the write-guard's PROTECTED list (Layer 2) protects the gate runner, lint/architecture config, git hooks, and CI workflows; and CI re-runs `validate --min-floor` with a hardcoded FLOOR so a weakened config can only ADD steps, never weaken canonical ones. The three enforcement layers (Stop hook, `pnpm validate`, CI) all read the same `tools/harness.config.mjs` so "done" means the same everywhere. Subagents are specialists with tool/model restrictions (read-only reviewers get `Read/Grep/Glob` only + `disallowedTools: Write, Edit`; authors get Edit/Write; models tiered opus vs sonnet by task). Commands orchestrate via `!`-prefixed inline command substitution and `$1` args. The skill enforces a locked migration→RLS→DAL→RSC→test→provenance→gate order with lazy-loaded references.

Roughly two-thirds of this is stack-agnostic (the hook contract, tamper-evidence layering, config-single-source-of-truth, CI floor mirroring, provenance gate, subagent/command/skill scaffolding, permission tiers). The remaining third is Next.js/Supabase-specific and must be re-mapped for the Tauri2+React19/Vite+Hono/Drizzle+self-hosted-Postgres stack: Supabase `getSession`/`getClaims`/`service_role`/RLS `auth.uid()` auth semantics → Entra ID OIDC + app-set Postgres session vars; the RSC/`use cache`/`proxy.ts`/CVE-2025-29927 model → a plain React SPA in a Tauri webview talking to a Hono server boundary; the flat `app/components/lib` root layout → the pnpm monorepo; Supabase CLI/MCP + `next build` gate steps → Drizzle migrations + Vite/Tauri builds; and the `supabase/functions` service-role carve-out disappears entirely.

transferable_patterns.name: Three-tier hook exit-code contract (shared I/O lib)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: A tiny Node-only module (no jq/bash) with readHookInput() (drains stdin, JSON.parse, returns {} on empty/parse error), and three terminal helpers encoding the contract: pass()=exit 0 (proceed); denyTool(event,reason)=exit 0 + stdout JSON {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName,permissionDecision:'deny',permissionDecisionReason}} (PreToolUse structured refusal); block(reason)=stderr + exit 2 (feeds message back to model). Every hook imports these so the semantics are uniform.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Copy verbatim — Node 22 built-ins only, zero deps, entirely stack-agnostic. It is the foundation every other hook builds on. Only change is cosmetic (hookEventName strings).

transferable_patterns.name: PreToolUse bash guard as regex tripwire
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Matcher 'Bash'. Reads tool_input.command, iterates a RULES array of [regex,message]; first match calls denyTool. Blocks rm -rf (permuted flag order), git push --force/-f/--force-with-lease, git reset --hard, fork bomb, plus secret/invariant leaks. Header explicitly states it is a tripwire not a sandbox; the settings deny list is the primary control.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Keep the destructive-shell rules verbatim. Swap the Supabase-specific rules (service_role, getSession, DROP TABLE) for stack rules: block reads of on-prem secret files, block dropping the privileged Postgres role, block bypassing FORCE RLS, block leaking Entra client secrets. The [regex,message] table pattern carries over unchanged.

transferable_patterns.name: PreToolUse write-guard: content-invariant blocking + two-layer tamper evidence
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Matcher Edit|Write|MultiEdit. (1) Resolves file_path RELATIVE to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR so a PROTECTED[] list of root-anchored (^…) regexes can match gate/config/CI files; if matched and HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT!=='1', deny with a human-in-the-loop message. (2) Exempts harness's own dirs (.claude/, tests, sanctioned dirs) and non-source files, then concatenates all candidate new-content fields (content/new_string/new_str/replacement/edits[].new_string) and runs content checks (banned key, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, context-sensitive checks gated on isClient/isServerArea path heuristics), plus a positive requirement that DAL files start with import 'server-only'.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: The PROTECTED root-anchored tamper list is the crown jewel — re-point it at the new gate surface (drizzle config, biome.jsonc, eslint flat config, tsconfig, pnpm-workspace.yaml, lefthook.yml, .github/workflows, tauri.conf.json, the validate runner). Keep CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR-relative resolution and the HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT escape hatch verbatim. Replace the Supabase content checks with: no privileged DB role in app code, no Entra secret in client bundle, server-only wall for packages/schema/Drizzle repo modules, no direct DB import from apps/desktop.

transferable_patterns.name: PostToolUse provenance gate (blocking)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Re-reads the just-written file from DISK, splits lines, and for lines matching a DECISION regex (RLS/auth/cache/tuning keywords) that are NOT comment lines, checks a 3-line lookback window for a `// SOURCE:` citation. Any decision site without one is collected; if the list is non-empty it writes them to stderr and exits 2, forcing the model to add citations and re-run /verify-citations.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Directly transferable. Swap the DECISION keyword regex for the new stack's decision sites (Drizzle policy/RLS SQL, Entra token verification, pgvector index/distance choices, llama.cpp inference params, retry/timeout/rate-limit consts). The comment-line skip and 3-line lookback logic stay identical.

transferable_patterns.name: PostToolUse fast single-file formatter (non-blocking)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Runs `biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched --colors=off <file>` on the single changed file via execFileSync; on failure surfaces Biome's notes to stderr but ALWAYS exits 0. Keeps the edit loop fast; heavy checks (tsc/eslint/knip/depcruise/build) live only on the Stop gate.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Copy as-is — the target stack already uses Biome. The 'fast per-file feedback, never block, defer heavy checks to Stop' split is a core performance pattern. Extend the extension allowlist for .rs (Tauri) if a rust formatter is wired similarly.

transferable_patterns.name: Unbreakable Stop gate driven by external config, with fallback + loop guard
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Stop matcher ''. Dynamically imports STOP_HOOK_STEPS from ../../tools/harness.config.mjs (falls back to a core step and WARNS if the import fails — never skips). Runs each [name,cmd] via execSync with maxBuffer 64MB (avoids ENOBUFS false-fails on large build output), collects the last 4000 chars of each failure, and exits 2 with all failures on stderr until green. Loop-guarded by stop_hook_active (changes the header when re-entered) and bounded by CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Structurally identical. Point STEPS at the new gate (drizzle migrate check, tsc, biome, eslint, vitest, tauri build, pgvector RLS-isolation suite). Keep the config-import-with-fallback, 64MB buffer, loop guard, and block cap. Crucially keep invoking runners DIRECTLY (node tools/validate.mjs), not via pnpm-script names.

transferable_patterns.name: Single-source-of-truth gate config + CI floor mirroring
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: harness.config.mjs exports VALIDATE_STEPS (what `pnpm validate` runs) and STOP_HOOK_STEPS (what Stop runs). validate.mjs runs them sequentially, stops on first failure. `--min-floor` merges a HARDCODED FLOOR (identical copies of canonical steps) so CI runs canonical commands even if the config was edited — a weakened config can ADD steps but never weaken the floor. The config file is itself write-guard-protected. All three layers (Stop hook, pnpm validate, CI) read the same config so 'done' is identical everywhere.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: This is the single most important transferable idea: one config, three consumers, plus a hardcoded CI floor that neutralizes config tampering. Re-implement wholesale with the new step commands. Keep --min-floor and --list flags.

transferable_patterns.name: Defense-in-depth permission tiers + deny-list self-protection
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: permissions has allow (read-only/local commands + read MCP tools + WebFetch domain allowlist), ask (network/remote-mutating ops), and deny (Layer-1 hard blocks: curl/wget/rm -rf/force-push/reset --hard/service_role, plus Read globs for .env*/~/.ssh, plus Edit+Write of .claude/hooks, settings.json, settings.local.json, .mcp.json, .harness/). defaultMode acceptEdits, disableBypassPermissionsMode 'disable', env sets the Stop block cap, and wires the hooks (with per-hook timeouts) and statusline.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Port the three-tier allow/ask/deny structure and the self-protecting deny globs (hooks/settings/mcp config must be non-editable by the agent) verbatim. Replace allow entries with pnpm/drizzle/tauri/vitest commands, ask with deploy/signing/Entra ops, and WebFetch domains with the on-prem allowlist (likely internal docs + llama.cpp; drop Supabase/Vercel/Next domains). Keep disableBypassPermissionsMode:disable.

transferable_patterns.name: Specialist subagents with tool + model restriction
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: 7 markdown agents with YAML frontmatter (name, description with 'MUST BE USED'/'PROACTIVELY' trigger language, tools, disallowedTools, model). Read-only reviewers (security-reviewer opus, torvalds-reviewer opus, accessibility-reviewer sonnet, citation-verifier sonnet) get Read/Grep/Glob (+WebFetch/MCP for the verifier) and explicit disallowedTools: Write,Edit; each reviews `git diff` vs base and ends with a fixed verdict token (PASS/FAIL, VERDICT: SHIP/BLOCK, CITATIONS: CLEAN/REJECTED). Authors (migration-rls-author, dal-author, test-author) get Edit/Write (and Bash except dal-author) and return file lists + exact commands.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Pattern is fully stack-agnostic: least-privilege tool grants, model tiering by task difficulty, and machine-parseable verdict lines that a command/loop can gate on. Re-author the domain content: security-reviewer audits Postgres16 FORCE RLS + Entra OIDC + pgvector isolation; dal-author→a Drizzle-repo/Hono-handler author; migration-rls-author→Drizzle migration author; keep torvalds/accessibility/citation reviewers nearly as-is.

transferable_patterns.name: Slash commands with inline command substitution + arg templating
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Markdown with frontmatter (description, argument-hint, allowed-tools, model). Bodies use `$1` for the argument and `!`-prefixed lines for inline shell substitution injected into the prompt (e.g. !`date +%Y%m%d`, !`git status --short`, !`git diff --name-only HEAD`). Header notes the command engine does NOT expand $(...), hence the ! form. new-feature.md orchestrates the whole vertical slice: invokes the skill, delegates layers to named subagents, requires reviewer verdicts, then /adr → /verify-citations.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Directly transferable authoring pattern. Keep adr and verify-citations essentially as-is (provenance is stack-agnostic). Rewrite new-feature's delegation graph for the monorepo (schema pkg → Drizzle migration → Hono handler → React screen → tests) and swap the git substitution lines as needed.

transferable_patterns.name: Skill with progressive-disclosure references + idempotent scaffold script
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: SKILL.md defines a strict 6-step order and instructs the agent to read a per-layer reference file (references/*.md) only when reaching that layer (keeps context lean). scripts/scaffold-slice.mjs (Node built-ins only) validates a kebab-case feature name, then writes stub files only if absent — idempotent. Migration idempotency is special-cased (fresh-timestamp filenames never existsSync-match, so it scans for any *_<feature>.sql). The DAL stub deliberately omits the literal banned key token so the write-guard won't block later edits to it.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Skill structure (locked order + lazy references + idempotent scaffolder) is fully reusable. Re-target the scaffold to monorepo paths (packages/schema, apps/server handlers/routes, apps/desktop screens, test files) and rewrite each reference for Drizzle/Hono/React/Tauri. Preserve the idempotency tricks and the 'avoid literal banned tokens in stubs' gotcha.

transferable_patterns.name: Always-loaded rules + advisory CLAUDE.md kernel
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: CLAUDE.md is the <200-line project-memory kernel (stack, commands, the validate contract, security invariants, size limits, provenance, spec-first governance) and explicitly states it is ADVISORY — the Stop hook + CI are the real enforcement. rules/ files are short; security-invariants and provenance are 'always loaded', while rsc-client-split uses a `paths:` frontmatter scope but self-documents that paths scoping is buggy/unreliable and defers hard enforcement to hooks/lint.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Keep the 'CLAUDE.md is advisory, hooks/CI enforce' philosophy and the always-loaded security+provenance rules. Rewrite invariants for the new stack (FORCE RLS via session var, Entra OIDC verification, no privileged role in app code, server/client boundary at the Hono API not RSC). Note the lesson that `paths:`-scoped rules are unreliable — put real invariants in always-loaded rules + hooks.

gotchas: Exit-code semantics are NOT interchangeable: PreToolUse refusals must be exit 0 + JSON permissionDecision:'deny' (denyTool), while PostToolUse/Stop blocking is exit 2 + stderr (block). Using exit 2 for a PreToolUse deny, or JSON for a Stop block, breaks the contract. hookio.mjs is the canonical encoding — reuse it, don't reinvent.

The write-guard resolves file paths RELATIVE to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR precisely so the PROTECTED list can be root-anchored (^tools/validate.mjs$ etc.); without this a nested node_modules/x/tools/validate.mjs would false-match. Preserve the CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR-relative resolution when porting.

Two DISTINCT tamper layers with different coverage: settings.json deny (Layer 1) protects .claude/hooks, settings*.json, .mcp.json, .harness/; the write-guard PROTECTED list (Layer 2) protects the gate runner/config/lint/CI/git-hook surface. The write-guard EXEMPTS all of .claude/, so the hooks themselves rely on the settings deny-list for protection — keep BOTH layers or a gap opens.

The Stop hook and STOP_HOOK_STEPS invoke runners DIRECTLY (node tools/validate.mjs, node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs, pnpm exec vitest) and NEVER via package.json script names — because redefining a script (e.g. "validate":"true") is an auto-accepted unguarded edit that would hollow out the gate. Direct invocation + write-guarding the runner files keeps the gate tamper-evident. Replicate this exactly.

CI runs validate.mjs --min-floor with a HARDCODED FLOOR duplicating the canonical steps, so editing harness.config.mjs can only ADD steps, never weaken the floor. The FLOOR array must be kept in lockstep with the config's canonical entries — that duplication is intentional, not a mistake to 'DRY away'. HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT=1 is the only human-in-the-loop escape hatch for protected files, and the agent cannot set its own env.

stop-validate-gate uses execSync maxBuffer 64MB specifically because next build / db reset output exceeds the 1MB default and throws ENOBUFS, which would masquerade as a step FAILURE on an otherwise-green build. Any large-output build/test step in the new stack needs the same buffer bump. The loop is also bounded by CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP (8) and detects re-entry via stop_hook_active to change its message; if the config import fails it WARNS and falls back but never skips.

posttool-source-check reads the file from DISK after the write (not from tool_input), only flags DECISION keywords that appear in actual code (it skips lines starting with //, *, /*), and scans a 3-line lookback window for // SOURCE:. So an explanatory comment mentioning a keyword won't false-trigger, but a decision line whose citation is >3 lines above will be flagged.

scaffold-slice.mjs has two subtle correctness tricks: (1) migration idempotency can't use existsSync because each run mints a fresh YYYYMMDDHHMMSS filename, so it scans the migrations dir for any *_<feature>.sql; (2) the generated DAL stub deliberately AVOIDS writing the literal banned service-role token, because the write-guard would then deny the agent's later full-file Write of that same stub. Both patterns must be reproduced for the new stub set.

The bash-guard is explicitly a substring/regex TRIPWIRE, not a sandbox — obfuscated or base64'd commands evade it. It is defense-in-depth on top of the settings permission model (allow/ask/deny), which is the primary control. Don't treat the guard regexes as a security boundary on their own.

rules/rsc-client-split.md self-documents that its `paths:` frontmatter scoping is buggy/unreliable and defers hard enforcement to always-loaded rules + hooks/lint. Lesson for the new harness: put real invariants in always-loaded rules and hooks, not in path-scoped rule files. The write-guard's isServerArea/isClient checks (getSession only banned server-side, DAL-import only banned client-side) are context-sensitive by path heuristic — re-derive those heuristics for the monorepo boundary.

summary:
The gate chain is a single-source-of-truth quality system. `tools/harness.config.mjs` exports two arrays of `[name, shellCommand]` tuples: `VALIDATE_STEPS` (the static gate run by `pnpm validate`) and `STOP_HOOK_STEPS` (what must pass before an agent turn may end). `tools/validate.mjs` imports `VALIDATE_STEPS` and runs them sequentially with `spawnSync(cmd, {shell:true, stdio:'inherit'})`, breaking on the FIRST non-zero exit, printing a per-step ✓/✗ summary, and exiting non-zero on any failure. The VALIDATE_STEPS order is: format (biome check) → types (tsc --noEmit) → lint (eslint) → provenance (check-sources.mjs) → route-boundaries → aliveness → dead-code (knip --strict) → architecture (depcruise) → build (next build), with two commented opt-in steps (styleguide, gated-routes). Three enforcement layers consume the same config so they cannot drift: (1) the Stop hook `stop-validate-gate.mjs` dynamically imports `STOP_HOOK_STEPS` from the config and runs each with `execSync` (64MB maxBuffer), exiting 2 with the last 4000 chars of failing output on stderr so the model must fix and continue — it invokes runners DIRECTLY (`node tools/validate.mjs`, `node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs`, `pnpm exec vitest`) never through package.json script names, so an agent can't redefine `validate` to `true`; (2) CI `quality-gate.yml` runs `node tools/validate.mjs --min-floor`; (3) lefthook git hooks run a fast subset pre-commit/pre-push. The `--min-floor` mechanism hardcodes a FLOOR array inside validate.mjs that is a verbatim copy of the canonical VALIDATE_STEPS; when `--min-floor` is passed, it runs FLOOR first, then any config-only extra steps whose names aren't already in the floor. Combined with the write-guard hook (which denies agent edits to harness.config.mjs, validate.mjs, all check-*.mjs, the lint/arch config surface, CI workflows, and the RLS runner unless `HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT=1`), this means editing the config can only ADD gates, never weaken the non-negotiable floor.

Each gate is a small standalone Node ESM script that exits 0/1. check-sources.mjs is the deterministic CI mirror of the PostToolUse `posttool-source-check.mjs` hook: both use an IDENTICAL `DECISION` regex (createPolicy|FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY|auth.uid()|getClaims|use cache|cacheLife|revalidate|maxRetries|timeoutMs|rateLimit) and a 3-line lookback window to require a `// SOURCE:` comment near every "decision site" in code (comments are skipped). The hook fires only during edits inside Claude Code; the gate re-runs the same heuristic over `git ls-files` on every PR. check-route-boundaries requires error.tsx + not-found.tsx for every route family (app root, route groups, UI-owning segments) with nearest-boundary inheritance semantics. check-aliveness-adoption and the two opt-in module gates (check-styleguide-manifest, check-gated-routes) all share a "manifest + union-scan" pattern: a data manifest file lists entries `{files, requires/label}`, the gate asserts every listed file exists and required import specifiers appear in the union of those files' sources, and (for styleguide/gated-routes) does a bidirectional check plus discovery scan so new gated surfaces are born accountable. check-types-drift regenerates Supabase types and diffs against the committed file, self-skipping only when no stack is reachable.

The RLS isolation suite is two independently-guarded layers behind `pnpm test:rls` → `tests/rls/run-rls.mjs`. Layer 1 is DB-backed pgTAP (`supabase db reset` with retry-once + `supabase test db`), run ONLY when `supabase/migrations` exists AND `supabase status` exits 0. Layer 2 is an SDK suite (`vitest run tests/rls`) that ALWAYS runs and self-skips via `rlsConfigured` (needs URL+anon+service keys AND ≥1 ISOLATION_TARGET). run-rls.mjs injects the local stack's URL+publishable+service keys from `supabase status -o env` (only setting vars not already present) so the SDK proof runs for real; it FAILS CLOSED in CI when migrations exist but the stack is unreachable (a skip there would be a false green). `client-factory.ts` defines `ISOLATION_TARGETS` (one entry per owner-scoped table), an admin client (service-role, seeding only), an anon client (RLS lens), `seedUser` (creates a GoTrue auth user + seeds one owned row so the positive control is non-vacuous), and `deleteUser` (FK cascade cleanup). `cross-tenant-isolation.test.ts` signs in as attacker A via a real GoTrue JWT and asserts: A sees its own row (positive control), A sees zero of victim B's rows on SELECT, cross-user UPDATE/DELETE affect zero rows (or error for append-only tables), and an INSERT smuggling B's id is rejected by WITH CHECK. The mid-turn `rls_verify` MCP server is a lighter probe: it connects via `pg` to SUPABASE_DB_URL inside a READ ONLY transaction that always ROLLBACKs, allow-lists table/column against information_schema before ever building SQL, confirms a seeded positive control (B owns ≥1 row, else SKIPPED-not-green), then `SET LOCAL ROLE authenticated` + sets request.jwt.claims to A and counts B's visible rows, returning ISOLATED/LEAK/SKIPPED — it never false-greens (any missing prerequisite returns SKIPPED, and CI's pnpm test:rls remains authoritative).

The provenance/citation pipeline runs end-to-end: authors write `// SOURCE: <authority> [corpus: <id>]` comments; the `corpus_search` MCP server does keyword lookup over a version-pinned `corpus/index.json` (returns `{id,version,url,snippet,sha256}` or honest NO_MATCH; `corpus_resolve` resolves an id) with a robust CORPUS_INDEX_URL override that ignores unexpanded `${...}` placeholders and http URLs and falls back to the local index rather than silently blanking; the PostToolUse hook flags missing citations live (exit 2); check-sources.mjs re-enforces in the gate + CI; a `/verify-citations` command + citation-verifier subagent (outside this slice) resolve ids through the corpus; and at release, the ci-provenance module emits SLSA L2 build provenance + SPDX SBOM via `actions/attest-build-provenance`/`attest-sbom` and VERIFIES the attestation in-CI. The opt-in modules each layer one concern: mutation (StrykerJS nightly full + per-PR incremental advisory ≥80%), ci-provenance (SLSA+SBOM on tags), ci-dast (Semgrep SAST + OWASP ZAP baseline), ci-lighthouse (perf+a11y budgets on public routes), gate-styleguide + gate-gated-routes (the two commented VALIDATE_STEPS with their manifests).

transferable_patterns.name: Config-as-single-source-of-truth gate list shared by hook + runner + CI
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: harness.config.mjs exports VALIDATE_STEPS and STOP_HOOK_STEPS as arrays of [name, shellCommand]. validate.mjs imports VALIDATE_STEPS and runs sequentially, break-on-first-failure, ✓/✗ summary, non-zero exit. The Stop hook dynamically imports STOP_HOOK_STEPS from the SAME file (with a hardcoded fallback if the import fails). CI runs the same validate.mjs. One list, three consumers, so 'done' means the same thing in the editor, at commit, and in CI.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Keep one tools/harness.config.mjs (or .ts run via tsx) at the monorepo root exporting the step lists. Replace step commands with the new stack: format→'biome check .' + 'eslint .', types→'tsc -b' across pnpm workspaces (apps/desktop, apps/server, packages/*), build→'pnpm -r build' (Vite build for desktop, tsc/tsup for Hono server), plus 'cargo check'/'cargo clippy' + 'tauri build' for the Rust/Tauri side. The Stop hook and a CI job both invoke node tools/validate.mjs. Because there is no Next build, the build step becomes vite build + tauri build + drizzle migration-compile.

transferable_patterns.name: --min-floor: hardcoded verbatim copy of canonical steps that CI runs so a weakened config can't weaken CI
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: validate.mjs contains a FLOOR constant that duplicates the canonical VALIDATE_STEPS. With --min-floor it runs FLOOR first, then appends only config steps whose names are not already in the floor. CI always passes --min-floor. So even if someone edits harness.config.mjs to drop or hollow a step, CI still runs the original command for that step name; config edits can only ADD steps.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Copy verbatim. Keep FLOOR = the non-negotiable subset (format/lint/types/build/provenance/rls). The mechanism is stack-agnostic — it is pure array merge by step name. Pair it with the write-guard so both the config file and validate.mjs are edit-protected; the FLOOR is the second line of defense if the guard is bypassed.

transferable_patterns.name: Direct-invocation Stop gate (no package.json script indirection) + exit-2 feedback loop
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: STOP_HOOK_STEPS invoke runners directly ('node tools/validate.mjs', 'node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs', 'pnpm exec vitest run') never 'pnpm validate', because an agent can freely edit package.json to redefine a script to `true`. The hook execSyncs each step (64MB maxBuffer to survive large build/docker output), collects failures, and on any failure writes a header + last 4000 chars of each failure to stderr and exit(2), which feeds errors back to the model. Loop-guarded by input.stop_hook_active and bounded by CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Same hook, same direct invocation. STOP_HOOK_STEPS for the new stack: ['validate','node tools/validate.mjs'], ['rls-isolation','node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs'] (now against self-hosted Postgres 16 with FORCE RLS), ['unit','pnpm -r exec vitest run']. Raise maxBuffer similarly for tauri build / cargo output. Keep the fallback-to-core-gate-on-import-failure so a broken config never silently skips the gate.

transferable_patterns.name: Deterministic CI mirror of a live PostToolUse hook (identical regex + window)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: The provenance check exists twice with an intentionally identical DECISION regex and 3-line lookback: the hook fires on each edit inside Claude Code (fast, local); check-sources.mjs runs the same heuristic over `git ls-files` in validate + CI so the rule is enforced on every PR, not only during agent edits. Both skip comment lines and test/generated files. Header comments explicitly say 'keep in lockstep'.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Keep both files. Update the DECISION regex to the new stack's decision sites: Drizzle policy/RLS builders (pgPolicy, sql`... USING`), Hono auth middleware, Entra OIDC token-validation constants (issuer, audience, jwks), llama.cpp inference params (temperature, n_ctx, timeout), retry/rate-limit consts. Replace the git-ls-files globs with the monorepo source globs (apps/**, packages/**). The dual-file lockstep pattern is the transferable idea.

transferable_patterns.name: Manifest + union-scan adoption gates (append-only data file + bidirectional checker)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: A pure-data manifest lists entries ({files, requires} or {file}). The checker asserts every listed file exists and that each required import specifier / declared primitive appears in the UNION of the entry's files' sources (so a primitive split across sibling files still passes). Styleguide and gated-routes add bidirectionality (every real ui file must be in the manifest and vice-versa) plus a discovery walk that flags any unlisted file matching a GATE_CALL regex — so a new gated surface must be registered or explicitly exempted with a reason. Regex gates are explicitly documented as heuristics, not proofs.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Directly reusable as Node scripts. For the desktop app, an aliveness manifest asserts React 19 islands still import selection/keyboard/optimistic kit hooks; a gated-routes-style gate asserts Entra-role-gated screens render a designed RestrictedState instead of a blank redirect. Point the walk at apps/desktop/src instead of app/. The union-scan + discovery + exemption-map shape is stack-independent.

transferable_patterns.name: Two-layer, fail-closed, self-skipping RLS isolation suite with seeded positive control
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Layer 1 pgTAP (structure: table exists, RLS ENABLED+FORCED, exactly N per-op policies) runs only when migrations exist AND a stack is reachable (with retry-once on flaky db reset). Layer 2 SDK suite mints real auth JWTs, then per owner-scoped target asserts: attacker sees own row (positive control, prevents vacuous pass on a deny-all DB), sees 0 victim rows on SELECT, UPDATE/DELETE touch 0 rows (or error if appendOnly), INSERT smuggling victim id is rejected by WITH CHECK. run-rls.mjs injects stack env from `status -o env`, only sets absent vars, and fails closed in CI when migrations exist but stack is down.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: On self-hosted Postgres 16 + FORCE RLS the pgTAP layer transfers almost verbatim (pgTAP runs against any Postgres via pg_prove or a psql harness — drop the supabase CLI wrapper, use a docker-compose'd postgres + `psql -f` or node-pg-migrate). The SDK layer becomes a Drizzle-client layer: instead of GoTrue JWTs, set the tenant via `SET LOCAL role` + `set_config('request.jwt.claims',...)` or your Entra-derived tenant claim, using two Drizzle connections (attacker/victim). Keep the positive-control seed and the fail-closed-in-CI guard. ISOLATION_TARGETS becomes a Drizzle-schema-driven list.

transferable_patterns.name: Never-false-green MCP probe: allow-list identifiers, READ ONLY + ROLLBACK, SKIPPED on any missing prerequisite
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: A stdio MCP server exposes rls_verify(table,tenantColumn,tenantA,tenantB). It refuses to interpolate any identifier not present in information_schema.columns (then double-quotes it), runs inside BEGIN; SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY; ... ROLLBACK, requires a seeded baseline (tenantB owns ≥1 row) or returns SKIPPED to avoid vacuous ISOLATED, drops to the RLS-subject role (SET LOCAL ROLE authenticated, never superuser), impersonates A via request.jwt.claims, and counts B's visible rows. Every failure path (no DB url, missing pg, unknown identifier, query error) returns SKIPPED(reason); only a completed probe returns ISOLATED/LEAK.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Reuse the whole safety discipline for a Hono/Drizzle stack. Point it at the self-hosted Postgres connection string (env var renamed from SUPABASE_DB_URL). Replace 'SET LOCAL ROLE authenticated' + request.jwt.claims with whatever RLS-subject role + tenant-claim mechanism the on-prem app uses (e.g. a 'app_user' role + set_config for the Entra tenant/oid). Keep the MCP server as an SDK-standard @modelcontextprotocol/sdk stdio server wired in mcp.json; it is Node, independent of Tauri.

transferable_patterns.name: Corpus-grounded provenance with resilient override handling
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: corpus_search / corpus_resolve MCP tools do keyword scoring over a version-pinned JSON corpus of {id,title,url,version,text,sha256}; return high-signal hits or honest NO_MATCH — the doctrine is 'never invent a source'. loadIndex() treats CORPUS_INDEX_URL as a FILE path, strips file://, and deliberately ignores an unexpanded ${...} placeholder or http(s) value (a real bug they hit where MCP hosts pass the template literal through), always falling back to the bundled index so a bad override never silently blanks the corpus. Stage-1 keyword match is behind a stable contract so it can be swapped for pgvector embeddings later.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: The new stack HAS pgvector + local llama.cpp, so implement Stage-2 directly: keep the identical tool contract but back corpus_search with pgvector similarity over embeddings generated by the local llama.cpp embedding endpoint, stored in the self-hosted Postgres. Keep corpus/index.json as the seed/source-of-truth and the fallback. Preserve the placeholder-ignoring override logic verbatim — it is a real-world footgun fix.

transferable_patterns.name: SLSA L2 build provenance + SBOM with in-CI attestation verification
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: On tag push, builds, packages the artifact, runs actions/attest-build-provenance (GitHub OIDC → Fulcio/Sigstore/Rekor) and anchore/sbom-action (SPDX) + actions/attest-sbom, then VERIFIES the attestation in-CI with `gh attestation verify` in a 5-attempt retry loop (attestation can lag). Header documents the honest limit: L2 proves build-path not source integrity (cites the Shai-Hulud stolen-OIDC incident); path to L3 is an isolated reusable workflow + SHA-pinned actions. All actions are already SHA-pinned; concurrency cancel-in-progress:false so a release attestation is never cancelled.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: For a signed Windows .exe this is the natural home for code-signing provenance. Keep attest-build-provenance over the built .exe/.msi (Tauri bundle) and the SBOM, and additionally attest the Authenticode-signed installer. Since it is on-prem/no-cloud you may run this on a self-hosted runner; keep the in-CI verify-the-attestation step and the SHA-pinning. Add cargo-auditable / cargo-sbom for the Rust side alongside the JS SBOM.

transferable_patterns.name: Two-tier CWV/a11y budgets with error-vs-warn split on public routes only
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Lighthouse CI builds+starts the app and asserts an assertMatrix scoped by matchingUrlPattern: accessibility/performance minScore 0.95 and LCP/CLS as ERROR (block), FCP/TBT/JS-byte size as WARN (lab≠prod, localhost transfer size isn't gzip-accurate). Only public routes are lab-tested; authed surfaces are covered by field RUM/web-vitals beacons. Placeholder public env lets next build render without a backend.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: A Tauri desktop app has no public web routes, so Lighthouse largely doesn't apply — but the error-vs-warn budget philosophy transfers to any perf gate (e.g. bundle-size budgets on the Vite output, or startup-time budgets on the packaged app). Keep the 'hard-fail on the few objective metrics, warn on the environment-sensitive ones' split. If a web admin surface exists on the Hono server, point lighthouserc at it.

transferable_patterns.name: Fast/slow tiered git hooks via lefthook (Biome-last ordering to avoid formatter fights)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: lefthook (single Go binary) runs pre-commit on staged files only (Prettier for Tailwind class sort at priority 1, then Biome authoritative at priority 2 with stage_fixed, then gitleaks that self-skips if the binary is absent), commit-msg (commitlint), and pre-push whole-repo (typecheck, eslint, knip, cspell) in parallel. Deliberately NOT parallel at pre-commit and ordered — running Prettier and Biome in parallel let Prettier's double-quote defaults re-stage over Biome and produce files that fail biome check in CI.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: The task already specifies lefthook + commitlint. Reuse this file. Since the new stack is Biome + ESLint 9 flat (no Prettier/Tailwind-class-sort described the same way — Tailwind v4), you can drop the Prettier priority-1 step and let Biome be sole formatter (simpler, no ordering hazard). Add pre-commit steps for `cargo fmt --check`/`clippy` on the Rust side and Drizzle-schema lint. Keep gitleaks self-skip and the pre-commit(staged)/pre-push(whole)/CI(authority) tiering.

transferable_patterns.name: Composable flat-config ESLint layer as a function (default-install vs retrofit spread)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: harness.eslint.mjs exports harnessEslint(options) returning a spreadable flat-config array; the default install does `export default harnessEslint()`, retrofits do `[...harnessEslint(), ...yourBlocks]`. It layers js.recommended + next configs + typescript-eslint strictTypeChecked/stylisticTypeChecked + jsx-a11y strict (recalibrated for scrollable regions) + eslint-plugin-boundaries (architecture) + security/no-secrets/no-unsanitized + house size limits (max-lines 200, max-lines-per-function 50 on app code only) + server/client invariant blocks (getSession ban, service_role ban, dangerouslySetInnerHTML ban, DAL import rules), with carefully scoped overrides because no-restricted-syntax options do not array-merge across flat blocks.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Keep the function-returning-array pattern for ESLint 9 flat config. Drop the Next/Supabase-specific blocks; replace with: React 19 + react-hooks + jsx-a11y for apps/desktop, Hono/node rules for apps/server, boundaries edges for the monorepo (packages/schema must not import apps; importer/eval isolation). Replace getSession/service_role bans with Entra-OIDC bans (e.g. no verifying tokens without checking issuer/audience, no raw DB connection in the desktop client) and Drizzle rules (no raw sql without RLS context). Keep the size limits and the 'Biome formats, ESLint lints' division. Per-package tsconfigRootDir via projectService.

stack_specific.name: next build as the final validate step
stack_specific.where: harness.config.mjs VALIDATE_STEPS build step + validate.mjs FLOOR + quality-gate.yml
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Replace with Vite build (apps/desktop), Hono server build (tsc/tsup), and `tauri build` for the signed .exe; the comment about 'build catches Cache Components prerender violations' has no analog — instead the build step catches Rust/TS type + bundler errors. Consider a separate cargo check/clippy floor step.

stack_specific.name: depcruise app components lib proxy.ts architecture command
stack_specific.where: harness.config.mjs, dependency-cruiser.js, quality-gate.yml, package.json arch script
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Repoint dependency-cruiser at the monorepo package roots (apps/desktop/src, apps/server/src, packages/schema, packages/importer, packages/eval). Replace the no-service-role-anywhere / no-client-to-dal / kernel-no-bespoke forbidden edges with monorepo boundaries: packages/schema must not import apps; desktop must not import server internals except via shared packages; importer/eval isolation.

stack_specific.name: Supabase client wrappers (lib/supabase/{client,server,proxy}.ts) and getClaims/getSession invariants
stack_specific.where: eslint harness.eslint.mjs server-invariants block, pretool-write-guard.mjs checks, corpus/index.json entries, CLAUDE.md
stack_specific.replacement_needed: No Supabase. Replace with Entra ID OIDC token validation invariants (verify issuer/audience/signature via JWKS; never trust unverified claims) and Drizzle DAL rules. proxy.ts (Next middleware) has no analog — Hono middleware is the request layer, and the 'proxy is not an auth boundary' rule maps to 'Hono edge middleware does optimistic checks; authorize in the DAL/route handler'.

stack_specific.name: service_role key bans across ESLint, dependency-cruiser, write-guard, bash-guard, settings deny
stack_specific.where: eslint no-restricted-syntax/properties, dependency-cruiser no-service-role-anywhere, pretool-write-guard checks, settings.json deny 'Bash(* service_role *)'
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Self-hosted Postgres with FORCE RLS has no service_role concept. Replace with a ban on the privileged/superuser DB connection string in app/desktop code (only the migration + seed tooling may use it), and a ban on bypassing RLS by connecting as a BYPASSRLS/owner role. Keep the tests/**-only carve-out for seeding.

stack_specific.name: supabase CLI orchestration (db reset, test db, status -o env, gen types, start)
stack_specific.where: tests/rls/run-rls.mjs, tests/a11y/run-a11y.mjs, tools/check-types-drift.mjs, tests/migrations/apply-rollback.mjs, quality-gate.yml
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Replace `supabase start/status/db reset` with docker-compose up of Postgres 16 + pgvector and a Drizzle migration apply (`drizzle-kit migrate` / push). `supabase status -o env` env injection becomes reading a local .env or docker connection string. `supabase gen types` drift check becomes a Drizzle schema→types drift check (drizzle-kit generate + git diff, or comparing generated types to committed). pgTAP still runs via pg_prove/psql against the compose Postgres.

stack_specific.name: supabase gen types drift gate (check-types-drift.mjs)
stack_specific.where: template/base/tools/check-types-drift.mjs, quality-gate.yml runtime job
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Drizzle is schema-first (TS is the source of truth), so 'generated types drift' inverts: instead verify migrations are in sync with the schema (`drizzle-kit generate` produces no new migration = no drift) and/or that the committed SQL snapshot matches. Self-skip logic (no stack → skip, stack up → real failure) transfers directly.

Keep the note that jsx-a11y strict is layered without re-registering the plugin only if some other config registers it; otherwise register once.

stack_specific.where: template/base/playwright.config.ts, tests/a11y/run-a11y.mjs
stack_specific.replacement_needed: A Tauri desktop app isn't a localhost web server. Replace Playwright webServer with either Playwright-driving-the-Hono-admin-surface, or Tauri's WebDriver (tauri-driver + WebdriverIO) for desktop E2E/a11y. The service-role user seeding via admin API becomes seeding via the privileged migration connection or a test-only Entra stub.

stack_specific.where: tests/rls/client-factory.ts, package.json.tmpl deps
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Replace supabase-js clients (admin/anon/signInWithPassword) with Drizzle/pg clients holding different Postgres roles/tenant claims, plus an Entra OIDC test-token minter (or a local JWKS stub) to simulate tenant A vs B. createAdminClient→privileged seed connection; createAnonClient→RLS-subject-role connection.

stack_specific.where: template/modules/ci-provenance/github/workflows/provenance.yml
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Subject becomes the signed Windows .exe/.msi Tauri bundle (plus SBOM for both npm and cargo deps). Drop the 'Vercel runs its own build pipeline' framing (on-prem, no cloud). May run on a self-hosted GitHub runner or an on-prem CI; keep OIDC-based attestation only if the on-prem CI supports Sigstore, else substitute an internal signing/attestation flow.

stack_specific.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_* placeholder env in CI jobs
stack_specific.where: quality-gate.yml, lighthouse.yml, provenance.yml env blocks
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Replace with the desktop/server build's required env (e.g. VITE_* public config, Hono server config, local Postgres DSN for test jobs). There is no publishable-key concept; Entra client/tenant IDs are the public config.

stack_specific.name: Biome domains next/react and Tailwind v4 at-rule exemptions
stack_specific.where: template/base/biome.jsonc
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Keep Biome as formatter/linter but set domains for react (desktop) and remove the 'next' domain; keep the Tailwind v4 noUnknownAtRules:off (Tailwind v4 is in the new stack too). The database.types.ts / supabase generated-file exclusions become the Drizzle generated migration/snapshot exclusions.

gotchas: Gate ORDER is load-bearing and cheap-to-expensive: format → types → lint → provenance → route-boundaries → aliveness → dead-code(knip) → architecture(depcruise) → build. validate.mjs breaks on the FIRST failure, so fast checks fail fast; keep the ordering when porting. STOP_HOOK_STEPS runs validate THEN rls-isolation THEN unit — a superset, not a duplicate.

--min-floor works by step NAME, not by command: FLOOR is merged first, and config steps are appended ONLY if their name isn't already in the floor. If you rename a canonical step in the config but not in FLOOR, CI will run BOTH (the floor's original command and the renamed config step). Names must stay identical between VALIDATE_STEPS and FLOOR.

The Stop hook and pre-commit both invoke runners DIRECTLY (node tools/validate.mjs, not `pnpm validate`) specifically so an agent can't neuter the gate by redefining a package.json script to `true`. Never route the Stop gate through a script name. package.json scripts exist only for human/CI ergonomics and are themselves not write-guard-protected.

check-sources.mjs and posttool-source-check.mjs MUST keep an identical DECISION regex and identical 3-line lookback window, or the live hook and the CI gate disagree (a decision site passes on edit but fails in CI or vice-versa). Both deliberately skip lines that START with //, *, or /* so a comment merely mentioning a keyword isn't flagged.

Every DB-dependent gate self-skips when no stack is reachable BUT fails closed in CI once migrations exist: run-rls.mjs exits 1 if CI + migrations present + stack unreachable, because a skip there would be a false green on the headline security gate. Preserve this asymmetry (skip pre-schema, hard-fail once there's something to test).

The RLS suites are non-vacuous by construction: both the SDK test and the rls_verify MCP probe require a SEEDED POSITIVE CONTROL (the victim owns ≥1 row / the attacker sees its own row) before asserting isolation — otherwise a deny-all or empty table reads as 'isolated' and hides a disabled-RLS bug. Any port must seed victim rows first and assert the attacker sees its own data.

rls_verify allow-lists table/column against information_schema.columns BEFORE building any SQL string and double-quotes the validated identifier; it never interpolates a raw name. It also runs READ ONLY + always ROLLBACK, and drops to the RLS-subject role (SET LOCAL ROLE authenticated) — probing as the login/superuser role would bypass policies and false-green. Every failure returns SKIPPED, never a green.

corpus-search-server.loadIndex deliberately ignores a CORPUS_INDEX_URL that is an unexpanded '${...}' placeholder or an http(s) URL, and falls back to the bundled index if an override path is unreadable — this fixes a real bug where MCP hosts pass the mcp.json env template literally and silently blanked the corpus. Don't 'simplify' this away.

The write-guard hook is the tamper-evidence layer: it denies agent Edit/Write to harness.config.mjs, validate.mjs, all check-*.mjs, tests/rls/run-rls.mjs, the whole lint/arch config surface, tsconfig, CI workflows, settings, and .mcp.json unless HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT=1. This is what makes 'config can only ADD gates' true. settings.json deny-list is layer 1; the write-guard is layer 2; --min-floor is layer 3. Port all three together or the guarantee breaks.

ESLint flat-config no-restricted-syntax options do NOT array-merge across blocks, so the server-invariant and client-invariant rules live in mutually-exclusive file globs and the a11y scrollable-region recalibration is a single-rule override; house size limits (max-lines 200 / max-lines-per-function 50) apply to app code only and are turned OFF for tests. Biome's noNoninteractiveTabindex is disabled so it doesn't fight the ESLint jsx-a11y recalibration, and useLiteralKeys is off because it conflicts with tsconfig noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature (process.env['X']). These cross-linter reconciliations must be carried over deliberately.

summary:
This slice is the packaging-and-doctrine layer of the harness: the zero-dependency Node installer (`installer/cli.mjs` + `commands/{init,update,doctor,enable}.mjs` + `lib/`), the two publish channels (npx/GitHub-template installer and the `.claude-plugin/` Claude Code plugin), the harness's own CI that proves the product works (`.github/workflows/{selftest,hygiene,lint,release,scorecard}.yml` + `scripts/{check-syntax,hygiene}.mjs` + `tests/`), the CI the template ships INTO projects (`template/base/github/workflows/*` + CODEOWNERS + renovate + zizmor), the doctrine docs, and the reference notes app under `template/stack/`.

The installer is a template-tree walker plus a manifest. `lib/copy.mjs#planTree` walks `template/{base,stack,modules/<name>}`, strips `.tmpl`, applies top-level dotless renames (`lib/layout.mjs#RENAMES`: `github`→`.github`, `gitignore`→`.gitignore`, etc.), and renders `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` tokens (`lib/placeholders.mjs`). Every installed file is classified by `lib/manifest.mjs#fileMode` into one of three ownership modes — `owned` (harness upgrades it), `seeded` (written once, project owns forever), `config` (seeded but hash-tracked for drift) — and recorded in `.harness/manifest.json` with a SHA-256 over the POST-render content. `init` detects bootstrap vs retrofit (`lib/detect.mjs`); on retrofit it merges `package.json` never-clobbering (`lib/merge-package-json.mjs`: a colliding `validate` script keeps the project's body and the harness version lands at `harness:validate`; existing dep ranges are kept, older majors flagged never downgraded), parks conflicting root configs as `<base>.harness.<ext>` siblings, skips all stack app code except an additive-seed allowlist, and rejects `src/` layouts. `update` upgrades only unmodified `owned` files, parks drift under `.harness/pending/` (unless `--force`), never rewrites `package.json`, never touches `seeded`. `doctor` re-hashes the tree read-only with CI exit codes (0 clean / 1 broken / 2 drift) and checks gate wiring. `enable`/`disable` flip module files and update the manifest.

Bootstrap-green-out-of-the-box is the release acceptance test, enforced by `selftest.yml`: a `bootstrap` matrix (Node 22/24) runs `init --yes` into a temp dir, `pnpm install`, then THE GATE (`pnpm validate` — all nine gates including `next build`), the unit suite, and a doctrine assertion that the RLS suite FAILS CLOSED in CI when migrations exist without a live stack; a `retrofit` job plants conflicts and asserts non-clobber semantics from the JSON report; `hook-contracts` and `installer-unit` jobs run the `tests/` node:test suites; a `runtime` job spins up a real Supabase stack and proves the RLS/pgTAP suite actually runs (not self-skips). The gate chain is a single source of truth (`template/base/tools/harness.config.mjs`) consumed identically by the Stop hook, `pnpm validate`, and CI, and CI runs `tools/validate.mjs --min-floor` which merges a HARDCODED floor so a locally-weakened config can add gates but never remove the non-negotiable ones. Release is tag-driven (`release.yml`): verify the CHANGELOG has a `## [x.y.z]` section, `npm pack`, `actions/attest-build-provenance` (SLSA L2) on the tarball, `gh release create`. The reference `template/stack/` notes app demonstrates the whole doctrine end-to-end (Supabase FORCE-RLS `notes` table with four per-operation initPlan policies + pgTAP + SDK cross-tenant isolation with a non-vacuous positive control, a server-only DAL returning DTOs, an optimistic-only `proxy.ts`, `getClaims()` everywhere, and a resolving `// SOURCE:` citation on every decision site).

transferable_patterns.name: Manifest-based file ownership with three modes + SHA-256 drift detection
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Every installed file is classified owned/seeded/config by path; init writes .harness/manifest.json with a sha256 of the rendered content. update upgrades only unmodified owned files, seeded are write-once, config is seeded-but-hash-tracked. doctor re-hashes read-only and reports drift with exit 0/1/2. Hashing over post-render content means per-project placeholder values never read as drift.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Carries almost verbatim. For the pnpm monorepo, key the manifest paths per workspace (apps/desktop, apps/server, packages/*). Ownership classes stay: harness configs (biome, eslint, tsconfig, drizzle.config, tauri.conf.json, vite.config) = owned/config; app scaffolding = seeded. doctor's wiring checks retarget from Supabase/Next files to Hono+Drizzle+Tauri equivalents.

transferable_patterns.name: Merge-never-clobber retrofit semantics
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: On retrofit, a colliding package.json script keeps the project's body and the harness version lands at harness:<name>; dep ranges are kept (older majors flagged via a cheap major-version rangeCovers, never downgraded); packageManager/engines set only if absent. Conflicting root configs land as <base>.harness.<ext> siblings with a conflict report; stack app code is skipped unless on an additive-seed allowlist and absent.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Directly reusable. In a monorepo, run the package.json merge per-workspace and also reconcile pnpm-workspace.yaml and the root package.json. Extend CONFLICTABLE with vite.config.ts, drizzle.config.ts, tauri.conf.json, biome.jsonc, eslint.config.js.

transferable_patterns.name: Dotless template storage + top-level RENAMES map
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Files that install to dot-paths are STORED dotless (github/, gitignore, mcp.json) because npm-packlist strips .gitignore/.npmrc and treats nested .gitignore as pack-ignore, and dotless template workflows can never execute in the harness's own Actions. RENAMES only applies at the tree top level. .claude/ is the ONE dotted exception, verified to survive npm pack. A lifecycle test asserts critical dotless paths ship in npm pack --dry-run.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Carries verbatim. Same npm-pack/self-execution hazards apply. Keep .claude/ dotted; store the shipped .github/ dotless. Add any new dotfiles (.node-version, .npmrc) to RENAMES.

transferable_patterns.name: Placeholder registry with bidirectional closure enforcement
transferable_patterns.where: installer/lib/placeholders.mjs (PLACEHOLDERS + render + TOKEN_RE), installer/lib/prompts.mjs, scripts/hygiene.mjs (closure), tests/installer/placeholders.test.mjs
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: A central registry declares each {{TOKEN}} with a prompt and a context-derived default. render() only substitutes registered tokens (unknown ones left intact for doctor/hygiene to flag) and deliberately does NOT match GitHub Actions ${{ }}. hygiene.mjs enforces closure both ways: every token used in template/ must be registered and every registered token must be used. --yes takes defaults, --set VAR=value overrides, else readline prompts (zero-dep).
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Reuse as-is. Swap the token set: keep PROJECT_NAME/PROJECT_SLUG/GITHUB_OWNER/SECURITY_OWNERS/DEFAULT_BRANCH; add ENTRA_TENANT_ID/ENTRA_CLIENT_ID, POSTGRES_DB_NAME, WINDOWS_SIGNING_SUBJECT, LLAMA_MODEL_PATH as needed. Keep the closure gate — it is what keeps the shipped artifact generic.

transferable_patterns.name: Single-source gate config + hardcoded CI floor (--min-floor) with direct-invocation tamper-evidence
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: One config declares the ordered [name, cmd] gate steps consumed by the Stop hook, pnpm validate, and CI. CI runs `validate.mjs --min-floor`, which merges a DUPLICATED hardcoded FLOOR ahead of config extras so editing the config can add gates but never weaken the non-negotiable ones. STOP_HOOK_STEPS invoke runners DIRECTLY (node tools/validate.mjs), never a package.json script name, so an agent can't redefine `validate` to `true`.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Carries directly and is the core doctrine. Swap step commands: format=biome, types=tsc max-strict, lint=eslint flat, build=vite build + tauri build (cargo), plus drizzle schema checks and RLS tests. Keep FLOOR as a lockstep duplicate of the universal steps; keep direct invocation.

transferable_patterns.name: Selftest matrix as the release acceptance test (bootstrap-green + retrofit-non-clobber + hook contracts)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Every PR/tag runs: installer-unit (syntax + hygiene + lifecycle tests), hook-contracts (pipe hook-event JSON to stdin, assert exit codes/deny behavior), a bootstrap matrix that init's into a temp dir and requires `pnpm validate` green out of the box, a retrofit job that plants conflicts and asserts non-clobber from the JSON report, and a runtime job with a live DB. This matrix IS the acceptance gate — the product's promise is proven, not asserted.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Reuse the whole matrix shape. The runtime job swaps supabase/setup-cli + `supabase start` for a Postgres 16 + pgvector service container and Drizzle migrations; hook-contract tests point at the new guard hooks; the bootstrap job additionally builds the Tauri app (headless) to prove the desktop scaffold compiles.

transferable_patterns.name: Leaked-string hygiene gate (shipped artifact must be generic)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Walks template/ and fails if any source-project identifier (org names, real GitHub handle, absolute /Users/ paths) or credential shape (JWT header.payload, Supabase sbp_/sb_secret_ tokens, non-local Postgres connection strings with creds) appears. A per-file ALLOWLIST permits deliberate mentions. Fails loudly if template/ is missing (a gate that scans nothing is a false green).
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Reuse the walker. Replace the leak patterns for the new org's identifiers and the new secret shapes: Entra client secrets, on-prem Postgres DSNs, Windows code-signing cert thumbprints, llama.cpp model paths. Keep the fail-if-empty guard.

transferable_patterns.name: Fail-closed runtime test runner (CI must not self-skip when the schema exists)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: The RLS runner self-skips honestly before a schema/stack exists, but `if (CI && haveMigrations && !reachable) exit 1` — in CI a skip on the headline gate would be a false green. The SDK isolation suite carries a non-vacuous POSITIVE control (seed each user a row so a deny-all DB can't pass every assertion vacuously). selftest asserts both the fail-closed path (nonzero + 'failing closed') and the real-run path ('running pgTAP checks' + '[rls] OK').
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Central to keep: self-hosted Postgres 16 with FORCE RLS means the same isolation doctrine applies. Replace Supabase CLI/GoTrue with raw Postgres roles + Drizzle-seeded fixtures and Entra-minted (or test-signed) JWTs; keep the CI fail-closed guard and the positive control.

transferable_patterns.name: Supply-chain-hardened shipped CI (SHA-pinned actions, harden-runner, CI-for-the-CI)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Every action pinned by full commit SHA with a version comment; step-security/harden-runner (egress audit) on every job that can host it; persist-credentials:false; actionlint + zizmor lint the workflows themselves; CodeQL security-extended, gitleaks full-history, OSV-Scanner (reusable workflows pinned by SHA, kept current by Renovate), OpenSSF Scorecard. Template workflows are stored dotless so they never run in the harness repo; lint.yml stages them into .fixtures for actionlint.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Reuse wholesale. On-prem changes little at the CI layer; add Rust/cargo-audit and cargo-deny for the Tauri crate, and a Windows signing/attestation job. Keep the dotless-storage-then-stage trick for actionlint.

transferable_patterns.name: Read-only doctor with CI-friendly exit codes and wiring probes
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: Re-hashes every manifested file, reports missing owned/config as errors and seeded/drift as warnings, and independently probes that the wiring still holds: package.json still runs the validate entrypoint, .claude/settings.json still wires the four guard hooks, harness.config.mjs still exports non-empty step arrays, and CLAUDE.md/CODEOWNERS have no unrendered {{placeholders}}. Exit 0 clean / 1 broken / 2 attention.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Carries directly; retarget the wiring probes to the new stack's entrypoints (Hono server bootstrap, Drizzle config, tauri.conf.json signing block) and the new hook set.

transferable_patterns.name: Opt-in module system (enable/disable) with file-drop vs uncomment-a-step distinction
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: enable copies a module's tree, records each file's module tag in the manifest; disable removes them unless locally modified. ci-* and mutation modules are pure file-drops (live on landing); the two validate-chain gate modules additionally require a human to uncomment their step in the write-guard-protected harness.config.mjs (HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT=1), and enable prints that hint. Tiers (core/standard/strict) are named module bundles.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Reuse. New modules could be ci-windows-signing, ci-sbom (SLSA on the .exe), mutation, gate-* for desktop-specific invariants; keep the file-drop vs config-uncomment split and the tier bundles.

transferable_patterns.name: Dual-channel packaging: npx/GitHub-template installer + Claude Code plugin manifest
transferable_patterns.where: .claude-plugin/plugin.json, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, package.json (bin + files + bootstrap script), installer/commands/init.mjs (--consume path)
transferable_patterns.how_it_works: The deterministic layer (hooks/gates/CI) ships via the installer (npx github:owner/repo, pinned to a tag) or GitHub-template `--consume` (installs in place then deletes installer/template/scripts/tests/.claude-plugin). The agent layer (subagents/commands/skill) ALSO ships as a Claude Code plugin whose plugin.json points agents/commands/skills at template/base/.claude/*, so the same source feeds both channels. plugin.json's description explicitly states the deterministic layer can't ship in a plugin.
transferable_patterns.adaptation_for_new_harness: Reuse both channels unchanged in shape. The plugin.json/marketplace.json point at the same template/base/.claude tree; the installer bin + files list + bootstrap/--consume flow are stack-agnostic.

stack_specific.name: Target detection (next dependency + src/ rejection + single-root layout)
stack_specific.where: installer/lib/detect.mjs, installer/commands/init.mjs
stack_specific.replacement_needed: detect() keys on a `next` dependency and hard-rejects src/ because every gate/glob assumes root-level app/ + lib/. The new harness is a pnpm MONOREPO (apps/desktop, apps/server, packages/schema|importer|eval), so detection must recognize pnpm-workspace.yaml + a Tauri/Vite/Hono signature instead of `next`, and the whole single-root assumption (app/, lib/, proxy.ts everywhere) must be re-expressed as workspace-relative paths.

stack_specific.name: Supabase CLI runtime job + pgTAP + GoTrue-minted JWTs
stack_specific.where: .github/workflows/selftest.yml (runtime job), template/base/github/workflows/quality-gate.yml (runtime job), template/base/tests/rls/*, template/stack/supabase/*
stack_specific.replacement_needed: Replace supabase/setup-cli + `supabase start`/`supabase db reset`/`supabase test db` + `supabase status -o env` with a self-hosted Postgres 16 + pgvector service container, Drizzle migrations, and pgTAP (or a Drizzle-driven RLS harness). JWT minting via GoTrue admin API becomes Entra ID OIDC tokens (or locally test-signed tokens) plus explicit Postgres role/SET-based session context for FORCE RLS.

stack_specific.name: next build gate (Cache Components prerender) + NEXT_PUBLIC_* placeholder env
stack_specific.where: template/base/tools/harness.config.mjs (build step), template/base/tools/validate.mjs (FLOOR), quality-gate.yml/selftest.yml env blocks, template/stack/next.config.ts
stack_specific.replacement_needed: The `next build` step exists specifically to catch Cache Components prerender violations. Replace with `vite build` + `tauri build`/`cargo build` (which catch Rust/bundling failures and signing config errors instead). The NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL/PUBLISHABLE_KEY placeholder env that lets `next build` run backend-less becomes whatever build-time env the Vite/Tauri build needs (likely none, or a Postgres DSN for server tests).

stack_specific.name: The reference stack app (Next App Router + Supabase clients + proxy.ts)
stack_specific.where: template/stack/app/*, template/stack/proxy.ts, template/stack/lib/supabase/{client,server,anon,proxy}.ts, template/stack/lib/dal/notes.ts, template/stack/package.json.tmpl, template/stack/vercel.json
stack_specific.replacement_needed: The notes app demonstrates doctrine via Next RSC + Server Actions + @supabase/ssr cookie clients + proxy.ts optimistic redirect. Rebuild as: a React 19 + Vite + Tailwind v4 + shadcn desktop UI (apps/desktop) talking to a Hono API (apps/server) whose Drizzle DAL is the authorization boundary and enforces FORCE RLS via a request-scoped Postgres session, with Entra OIDC replacing Supabase auth. Drop vercel.json; add tauri.conf.json + the signed-.exe pipeline.

stack_specific.name: Supabase auth security invariants (getSession/getClaims/service_role)
stack_specific.where: template/base/docs/harness/README.md (security invariants + Edge-Function carve-out), template/base/.claude/rules/security-invariants.md, template/base/CLAUDE.md, README.md
stack_specific.replacement_needed: The non-negotiables are Supabase-specific: never server-side getSession(), always getClaims(), never SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, proxy.ts CVE-2025-29927. Re-express for Entra OIDC (validate ID/access tokens against Entra JWKS, never trust unverified claims; app_roles from Entra, not user-editable attributes) and a self-hosted Postgres privileged role (the service_role analogue that bypasses RLS) that is banned outside a governed migration/admin path. RLS itself (ENABLE + FORCE + initPlan) carries directly to Postgres 16.

stack_specific.name: CONFLICTABLE / additive-seed config lists (Next+Supabase file names)
stack_specific.where: installer/lib/layout.mjs (CONFLICTABLE, RETROFIT_ADDITIVE, SEEDED_FILES/PREFIXES)
stack_specific.replacement_needed: These lists enumerate Next/Supabase-shaped paths (eslint.config.mjs, next.config.ts, lib/supabase/*, supabase/, vercel.json). Rewrite for the new stack: vite.config.ts, tauri.conf.json, drizzle.config.ts, apps/*/package.json, packages/schema/*, plus keep the shared ones (biome.jsonc, tsconfig.json, lefthook.yml, commitlint.config, playwright.config).

stack_specific.name: SLSA build-provenance attestation over an npm tarball / .next build
stack_specific.where: .github/workflows/release.yml, template/modules/ci-provenance/github/workflows/provenance.yml
stack_specific.replacement_needed: release.yml attests a `npm pack` tarball; the ci-provenance module attests a .next tarball + SPDX SBOM. For a signed Windows .exe the attestation subject becomes the signed .exe/MSI, and the release adds Authenticode signing (and ideally attests the signing). The gh-release + CHANGELOG-section gate carries unchanged.

stack_specific.name: Local llama.cpp inference vs remote-model corpus grounding
stack_specific.where: template/base/docs/harness/README.md (provenance pipeline / corpus), docs/security/approved-tools.md, template/base/.claude/settings.json (WebFetch allowlist, MCP servers)
stack_specific.replacement_needed: The provenance corpus + WebFetch doc-domain allowlist assume online docs and a hosted model. For on-prem + local llama.cpp, the version-pinned local corpus (no network) fits even better, but the WebFetch allowlist may be removed entirely and MCP servers must be fully local/offline; approved-tools policy tightens to no-egress.

gotchas: Manifest hashes are computed over POST-render content (installer/lib/manifest.mjs), so per-project placeholder substitutions never register as drift in doctor/update — hash after rendering, not before.

RENAMES apply ONLY at the tree top level (copy.mjs: name mapped only when relInstall===''); nested gitignore/github names are left literal. .claude/ is the single dotted directory shipped as-is (verified to survive npm pack); everything else dot-pathed is stored dotless.
File permission bit is decided purely by content.startsWith('#!') → 0o755 else 0o644 (init.mjs/update.mjs/enable.mjs write()). Shebang hooks/scripts must keep their shebang or they lose the exec bit.

Placeholder resolution order is load-bearing: PLACEHOLDERS is iterated in declaration order (NAME, SLUG, OWNER, SECURITY_OWNERS, BRANCH) and later defaults read earlier ANSWERS (PROJECT_SLUG derives from answers.PROJECT_NAME; SECURITY_OWNERS from answers.GITHUB_OWNER). Reordering breaks the derived defaults. render() also must NOT match GitHub Actions ${{ }} — TOKEN_RE is /{{([A-Z0-9_]+)}}/ (uppercase, no leading $).

The --min-floor FLOOR in tools/validate.mjs is a hand-DUPLICATED copy of the canonical VALIDATE_STEPS and must be kept in lockstep manually; it deliberately OMITS the project-shaped gates (route-boundaries, aliveness) and keeps only the universally-passing ones (format, types, lint, provenance, dead-code, architecture, build) so CI's floor never blocks a fresh install.

The Stop gate invokes runners DIRECTLY (STOP_HOOK_STEPS = node tools/validate.mjs / node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs / vitest), never `pnpm validate`, precisely so an agent editing package.json (an auto-accepted, unguarded edit) cannot hollow out the gate. Preserve direct invocation when porting.

update() never rewrites package.json — it was merged only at init; it only emits NOTES about new/changed upstream scripts. Seeded files are never overwritten by update; owned files upgrade only when the on-disk hash equals the recorded hash, otherwise the incoming version is parked under .harness/pending/<path> (unless --force). retrofit skips ALL stack files except RETROFIT_ADDITIVE ones that are absent.

RLS runner fail-closed contract: run-rls.mjs exits 1 when CI && migrations exist && stack unreachable (a skip there is a false green), but self-skips green locally in the same state. selftest's bootstrap job asserts the nonzero exit AND greps for 'failing closed'; the runtime job asserts it actually ran ('running pgTAP checks' + '[rls] OK'). The cross-tenant suite needs a seeded POSITIVE control or a deny-all DB passes every assertion vacuously.

Template CI workflows live under template/*/github/workflows/ stored dotless SPECIFICALLY so GitHub never executes them in the harness repo's own Actions; lint.yml copies them into .fixtures/tpl/.github/workflows/ to actionlint them. OSV reusable workflows and the semgrep container job are pinned by SHA-with-version-comment and canNOT host harden-runner (documented exception).

Release gates on an exact CHANGELOG section: release.yml greps for `## [<tag-without-v>]` and awk-extracts that block as the release notes — a missing/misnamed section fails the release. The stack ships a default_grants migration timestamped BEFORE the first table migration to restore public-schema GRANTs that newer Postgres images drop (otherwise every API call fails with permission-denied before RLS is even consulted).
